channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI slop has is still distorting news about the L.A. wildfires
item.description: A firefighter emerges from the flames, a bear cub tucked under each arm. Another, in full protective gear, carries a tiny puppy away from the inferno. A third cradles a baby deer close to their chest. This clip posted to Instagram by @FutureRiderUS last week has over 43 million views and counting.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Biden takes big step for the future of AI with ambitious executive order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US TikTok users flock to Chinese app Xiaohongshu in protest with TikTok ban looming
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — As the threat of a TikTok ban looms, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What were the biggest stories from CES? Tune in to our podcast to find out
item.description: We round up our favorite moments from the Las Vegas tech expo, from the big trends coming in 2025 to the 'weird stuff'.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5050 was missing in action at CES 2025 – but the budget GPU might just have been spotted in a surprising laptop
item.description: Want a Blackwell GPU to pep up cheaper gaming laptops? Here’s some good news – Nvidia’s RTX 5050 has been spotted (but not in a gaming notebook).
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI outlines proposals to bolster national security, drive economic growth
item.description: OpenAI laid out a series of new artificial intelligence (AI) policy proposals Monday, encouraging U.S. development of the emerging technology to stay ahead of China while minimizing its risks.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Best cutting-edge health and fitness tech from CES 2025
item.description: CES 2025 showcased everything from artificial intelligence-powered sleep aids to smart ear-cleaning devices. Kurt the CyberGuy has the details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness
item.description: Current and former executives say the federal technology themes for 2025 will be similar to 2024 with the addition of efficiency and effectiveness. The post For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tech unemployment in the US drops to lowest level in more than two years
item.description: Tech hiring rose in December, dropping the IT unemployment rate to 2% — its lowest since November 2023, according to an analysis of the latest jobs data published today by the US Bureau of Labor statistics (BLS). The overall national unemployment rate held steady at 4.1%, according to the BLS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From AI assistants to holographic displays, automakers showcase in-cabin experiences at CES
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Interior lighting aimed at reducing motion sickness. Shiny holographic dashboards displayed on windshields. And AI-powered voice assistants paired with infotainment systems to keep you company on long drives.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Working with minimal APIs in .NET
item.description: If you’re building cloud-native applications with microservices, they need to be small. You don’t want code that comes with enough dependencies and required libraries to turn something that should be able to scale in milliseconds into megabytes of code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS to invest $11 billion in Georgia to expand infra for gen AI
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is planning to invest $11 billion in the state of Georgia to expand its infrastructure in the US to support the growing demand for generative AI, AI, and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
